perturbation.original_text: 1.2 The Parties agree hereby that Confidential Information needs not to be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitutes a trade secret in order to be designated Confidential Information and therefore protected.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 The Parties agree hereby that Confidential Information must be demonstrated to be novel, unique, or constitutes a trade secret in order to be designated Confidential Information and therefore protected.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'needs not to be' to 'must be demonstrated to be' directly contradicts the original intent. This creates a higher burden of proof for the Disclosing Party to show that the information is novel or unique, thereby contradicting section 1.1 that states Confidential Information means any data or information that is pr oprietary to or possessed by a P arty and not generally known to the public or that has not yet been revealed, whether in tangible or intangible form, whenever and however disclosed, including, but not limited to..
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential I nformation means any data or information that is pr oprietary to or possessed by a P arty and not generally known to the public or that has not yet been revealed, whether in tangible or intangible form, whenever and however disclosed, including, but not limited to: (i) any scientific or technical information, invention, design, process, procedure, formula, improvement, technology or method; (ii) any concepts, ideas, samples, reports, data, know -how, works -in-progress, designs, drawings, photographs, development tools, speci fications, software programs, source code, object code, flow charts, and databases; (iii) any marketing strategies, plans, financial information, or projections, operations, sales estimates, business plans and performance results relating to the Party’s past, p resent or future business activities, or those of its affiliates, subsidiaries and affiliated companies; (iv) trade secrets; plans for products or services, and customer or supplier lists; (v) any other information that should reasonably be recognised as Confide ntial Information by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'needs not to be' with 'must be demonstrated to be'. This directly contradicts the original agreement by imposing a requirement for Confidential Information to be novel, unique, or constitute a trade secret. This new requirement conflicts with the contradicted text, which defines Confidential Information as any data or information proprietary to a Party and not generally known, irrespective of its novelty or uniqueness. This introduces a contradiction by narrowing the scope of what qualifies as Confidential Information, creating a higher burden of proof and potential unenforceability. It aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because it inconsistently defines the criteria for Confidential Information, creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its em ployees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confide ntial quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence, the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees who are part of the ILDA-care project team that need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confide ntial quality of the information provided. The Receiving Party will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'representatives or agents' from those allowed access, and removing the need to obtain their agreement, this directly contradicts the part of 3.2 which states the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents. This introduces a contradiction, because the recipient is only liable for personnel
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees who are part of the ILDA-care project team that need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confide ntial quality of the information provided. The Receiving Party will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the scope of individuals for whom the Receiving Party is responsible. The original text states liability for 'personnel representatives or agents', while the changed text limits this to only 'personnel'. The contradicted text reflects this limited scope as well. Because of this removal, and according to the explanation, this change creates a direct contradiction with the original scope outlined in clause 3.2, introducing ambiguity about liability for representatives and agents, thus causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all readily accessible copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the word 'readily accessible', it introduces ambiguity on the accessibility of the copies to be retrieved, and contradicts section 3.8 which states Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies', whereas the changed text modifies this to 'The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all readily accessible copies'. The addition of 'readily accessible' creates ambiguity regarding which copies must be returned or destroyed. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'the Receiving Party may retain such of its documents as required to comply with mandatory law, provided that such Confidential Informat ion or copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidentiality obligation'. Because 'all copies' is now limited to 'readily accessible copies', it implies that other copies don't need to be returned, but also creates ambiguity with documents that must be retained to comply with mandatory law. Therefore, this constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as it weakens the initial obligation and introduces uncertainty in conjunction with section 3.8.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.1 This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force until the ILDA -care project is finalised . Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that wa s disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.1 This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force for a period of two years from the date of signing . Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that wa s disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term from 'until the ILDA -care project is finalised' to 'for a period of two years', it creates a contradiction. It creates a situation where the agreement may end after two years, but the confidentiality obligations are indefinite, contradicting the temporal boundary of the agreement itself. If ILDA-care project runs for 5 years, but the agreement is valid for only 2, it creates a conflicting message to the obligations. Section 4.1, says: This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force for a period of two years from the date of signing . Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that wa s disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement remains in force until the ILDA-care project is finalized. The changed text limits the agreement to a period of two years. The explanation explicitly states that if the ILDA-care project extends beyond two years, a conflict arises, because the confidentiality obligations remain indefinite despite the agreement expiring. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty about the duration of the agreement and its relationship with the confidentiality obligations, making it unclear whether the agreement ends after two years regardless of the project's status, or whether the confidentiality obligations still apply even after the agreement's expiration. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', where obligations are defined inconsistently, creating enforcement difficulties.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Competition The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will not prevent or in any way limit either Party from developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other; or providing products or services to others who compete with the other Party; as long as those results have not become from a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Competition The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will allow either Party from developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other; or providing products or services to others who compete with the other Party; as long as those results have not become from a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will not prevent or in any way limit either Party from' to 'will allow either Party from', it suggests an endorsement or permission for competitive activities, which contradicts the underlying purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. This conflicts with 3.3 The Receiving Par ty will use the Confidential Information exclusively for the permitted purpose stated in clause 2 and not use the information for its own purposes or benefit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will use the Confidential Information exclusively for the permitted purpose stated in clause 2 and not use the information for its own purposes or benefit.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the receipt of confidential information will 'not prevent or in any way limit' competitive activities. The changed text alters this to 'will allow' competitive activities. This change creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which mandates that the Receiving Party use the Confidential Information exclusively for the permitted purpose and 'not use the information for its own purposes or benefit'. By explicitly allowing competitive activities, the changed text conflicts with the restriction on using confidential information for the Receiving Party's own benefit, thus introducing a strong in-text contradiction. This adheres to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, as the changed text creates ambiguity regarding the permitted use of confidential information, making enforcement difficult.
